agentic-workspace.html.title: Agentic-workspace
agentic-workspace.html.description:
#Tools/ai Artificial intelligence is rapidly shifting from conversational chatbots to autonomous, action-oriented agents. This repository is a curated master-guide for developers, engineers, and tech enthusiasts looking to build, optimize, and deploy these next-generation workflows.What you will find in these notes: Anthropic's Ecosystem: Mastering the CLI-based Claude Code. Google's Developer Tools: Exploring cutting-edge IDEs like Anti-Gravity, Opal, and Gemini-CLI. Workflow Automation: Building hands-free systems (like cybersecurity threat feeds) using n8n. Practical Integrations: Deploying parallel AI models and leveraging open-source skills.
Pro-Tip on Tooling & Pricing: Unlocking the absolute full potential of these workflows is highly accessible. Premium developer tiers have standardized around a ~$20/month price point—specifically, Claude Pro ($20/mo) and Google AI Pro/Gemini Advanced ($19.99/mo)Investing in these baseline subscriptions provides immense value, granting you access to flagship reasoning models, extended context windows, and advanced integrations (like Google Workspace and NotebookLM). Together, they provide the foundation to turn AI from a simple assistant into a highly capable co-worker. official courses by anthropic for full understanding Claude in various topics with Certification This Claude Code masterclass provides a four-part blueprint for building applications at scale by mastering advanced command-line hacks (such as custom plugins and phased planning), utilizing both web and local interfaces for efficiency, rapidly replicating complex tools using API documentation, and relying on thorough planning and iteration to build real-world projects regardless of your technical background The performance optimization system for AI agent harnesses. From an Anthropic hackathon winner.Not just configs. A complete system: skills, instincts, memory optimization, continuous learning, security scanning, and research-first development. Production-ready agents, hooks, commands, rules, and MCP configurations evolved over 10+ months of intensive daily use building real products
https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-codeResearch these Topics based on your need to Build them Research Claude Agents Research Claude Sub-agents Research Claude Co-work
Research Claude Code Plugins - Claude code Plugins Guide Claude just released a Chrome extension that lives in your browser.
It can see whatever page you’re on, answer questions about it, and even take actions for you. But the best feature is the workflow recorder—show Claude how you do something once and it saves it as an AI automation that runs on its own whenever you want. If you spend 20 minutes a day on repetitive tasks, just show Claude once and it will do it for you. This free AI Chrome extension is a game changeUse Safely
Browser AI can encounter prompt injection hidden instructions on websites that attempt to hijack Claude's actions. We've built defenses and tested extensively, but recommend these practices: Start with trusted sites: Grant permissions to familiar websites first Review sensitive actions: Always confirm before Claude handles financial, personal, or work-critical tasks Report unexpected behavior: Help us improve by flagging issues through feedback options Full safety guide: https://clau.de/getting-started-with-claude-for-chrome • Jules - AI coding agent (GitHub integration, builds features, fixes bugs)ANTIGRAVITY AI IDE that can deploy parallels Agents to build Your SaaS / Project
Anti-Gravity Masterclass contains full guide setup MCP's agents , skills etc.... 3 hour Course
Google introduced mini apps deployed just by writing to execute workflows but limited to Google eco-system
• Stitch - Wireframes → UI + code instantly used for User interface Design
Gemini cli coding agent , access Gemini with cli also it has some features like Claude Code like bug report Skills extensions memory MCP
sudo npm install -g @google/gemini-cli@latest It's Gemini playground for developers testing it Capabilities og Gemini AI model , try out prompts and many more this tool standalone can be your tutor/researcher/note keeper it has capabilities to analyze data from multiple resources (pdf , YouTube Video's , Links etc...) & Client can ask about information to gain knowledge etc.. this AI tool that can integrate with agent's to provide Powerful research Capability if integrated with any Agentic model like Gemini or Claude code Now this tool integrated with Gemini u can access the notes / research that u Done with Gemini ai model to preform the agentic task based on research/notes that is created using NotebookLM
example Claude Code + NotebookLM + Obsidian , The workflow integrates Claude Code, NotebookLM, and Obsidian to create a self-improving research system that searches YouTube, analyzes data, generates deliverables, and organizes everything in an Obsidian vault, adapting to user preferences over time
AI coding agent uses Gemini with your GitHub Repo, builds features, fixes bugs etc.. Build test project in GitHub , use Jules to write the code and Manage the Repository
Jules Overview
Automation of Workflows using AI Must learn and Deploy Automated Workflows Related To your Major or Job Role combining AI tools Examples Gemini-cli with Claude Code with same Project Anti-Gravity with Claude code Claude code with notebookLM Collection of system prompts/system messages/developers messages.
System Prompts 1
System Prompts 2
Skills are sets of instructions. They turn your AI agent into an expert. Like crafting clean front-end designs. Or building mobile apps. Writing sharp copy. Running SEO checks. And more. You don't build them yourself. released skills.sh. It's free. Over 80,000 skills. Anyone can grab them. I tried it last week. Asked my AI for a Next.js landing page. Picked a skill from the site. Ran one command. Results? Clean code. Fast. Like handing your AI a toolbox it already knows. Here's how easy: Go to skills.sh. Pick one. Say, for e-commerce. Run npx skills install . Your AI gets better. Right away. i use these free models for brainstorming projects build mini projects/tools for your daily use or Build Scripts.

KIMI K2 - k2.5 instant
Manus - the Autonomous agent in browser
CURSOR - agentic IDE What is Atoms (formerly MGX)? A next-generation AI business team that helps build apps using AI. What is Race Mode? A feature that fundamentally changes how AI generates code. Deploys multiple advanced AI models simultaneously (e.g., GPT5, Claude Sonet 4) to work on the exact same task. Aims to provide faster, more reliable results by generating multiple outputs at once. How Race Mode Works: Parallel Processing: Instead of one AI guess, four different AI models (up to four open-source models like Deep See V3) run in parallel. Diverse Approaches: Each model approaches the challenge differently, focusing on areas like design, performance, or feature completeness. Evaluation System: After completion, an evaluation system scores each result based on code quality, functionality, design, consistency, and reliability. Ranked Options: The top four options are presented in a ranked list, allowing users to preview and choose the best one. Cost-Effective: Offers high-quality results at a significantly lower cost compared to single top-tier models like GPT4. Flexibility: Users can toggle individual models on/off and choose a "global creativity level" (balanced or creative). Real-time Monitoring: A working interface with four panels shows each AI engine coding independently, allowing users to watch them work in parallel and check runtime status. Notifications: Atoms sends a notification once the race is complete, so you don't have to wait. Selection & Refinement: The AI suggests the best-performing build, but users can select any option. The chosen project then loads for further refinement, editing, and integration. Publishing & Sharing: Easy one-click publishing to a connected domain with a unique link, and simple sharing options to social platforms. Benefits of Race Mode: Confidently choosing the best solution from multiple options. Saves time by eliminating repeated prompt regeneration. Eliminates frustration and gives confidence in the final result. Provides transparency, control, and multiple options instead of a single output.

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_displayTitle.description: Descriptive title to show above the feature

info_showCustomIcons.description: Show custom icons for files and folders

info_showDefaultFolderIcons.description: Show a default icon of a folder for every folder in the tree

info_showDefaultFileIcons.description: Show a default icon of a file for every file in the tree

info_defaultFolderIcon.description: The icon to use for folders. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultFileIcon.description: The icon to use for files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultMediaIcon.description: The icon to use for media files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_exposeStartingPath.description: Whether or not to show the current file in the file tree when the page is first loaded

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_displayTitle.description: Descriptive title to show above the feature

info_showOrphanNodes.description: Show nodes that are not connected to any other nodes.

info_showAttachments.description: Show attachments like images and PDFs as nodes in the graph.

info_allowGlobalGraph.description: Allow the user to view the global graph of all nodes.

info_allowExpand.description: Allow the user to pop-out the graph view to take up the whole screen

info_attractionForce.description: How much should linked nodes attract each other? This will make the graph appear more clustered.

info_linkLength.description: How long should the links between nodes be? The shorter the links the more connected nodes will cluster together.

info_repulsionForce.description: How much should nodes repel each other? This will make disconnected parts more spread out.

info_centralForce.description: How much should nodes be attracted to the center? This will make the graph appear more dense and circular.

info_edgePruning.description: Edges with a length above this threshold will not be rendered, however they will still contribute to the simulation. This can help large tangled graphs look more organised. Hovering over a node will still display these links.

info_minNodeRadius.description: How small should the smallest nodes be? The smaller a node is the less it will attract other nodes.

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.

info_allowResizing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be resized

info_allowCollapsing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be collapsed

info_rightDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the right sidebar

info_leftDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the left sidebar

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_sourcePath.description: The local path to the source .html file which will be included.

info_allowFoldingLists.description: Whether or not to allow lists to be folded

info_allowFoldingHeadings.description: Whether or not to allow headings to be folded

info_siteUrl.description: The url that this site will be hosted at

info_authorName.description: The name of the author of the site
